item.title: Version 0.1.252
item.description: Release v0.1.252

item.title: Version 0.1.251
item.description: FIX (for real): Sync Health no longer freezes the app. v0.1.250 moved the work to a background thread, but container.fetchShares() blocks at the Core Data persistent-store-coordinator LOCK when the CloudKit mirror is wedged — so it froze the whole app regardless of thread. This version removes fetchShares from the health check entirely. The census that matters — CloudKit-vs-local, per-workspace store + lead counts, per-store totals, stranded records — needs none of it. 'Shared' is now detected by store membership (a workspace mirrored into the .shared store is one shared with you), which is exactly the signal for diagnosing whether a Mac is truly receiving a teammate's share vs holding a separate local copy. Detailed participant info stays in the Team workspace section, opened deliberately. The panel now opens instantly and can't lock up.

item.title: Version 0.1.250
item.description: FIX: app froze when opening Sync Health. The panel ran fetchShares() and the lead/workspace fetches synchronously on the main thread — and fetchShares blocks on a wedged CloudKit mirror, which froze the whole UI exactly when sync was broken (when you most need the panel). All of it now runs on a background context; the panel shows a spinner until the report is ready. Functionally identical output, just non-blocking.

item.title: Version 0.1.249
item.description: Sync error diagnostics: exhaustive cause dump. A generic 'Cocoa 134060' export failure reported 'see the underlying error for the cause' but then showed nothing beneath it — because that error stashed its real detail under non-standard userInfo keys (affected objects, conflict lists, validation/CloudKit internals) the interpreter didn't check. It now dumps EVERY remaining userInfo key (affected NSManagedObjects summarized by entity, nested errors recursed, other values truncated) so the actual cause is always visible in the Copy Diagnostics output. No behavior change to sync itself.

item.title: Version 0.1.248
item.description: Cross-device EDITS now sync, not just new records. Root cause of 'same lead count, but a follow-up logged on one Mac doesn't update the status on the other': the Core Data merge policy was mergeByPropertyObjectTrump, which makes the LOCAL in-memory copy win a conflict — so when the other Mac imported your edit (incremented followUpCount, new lastFollowUpDate), its stale local copy trumped and discarded the incoming change. New records had no conflict so they synced; updates to existing leads got clobbered. Switched to the canonical NSPersistentCloudKitContainer policy, mergeByPropertyStoreTrump (remote/server wins = last-writer-wins across devices), so edits propagate. The objectTrump workaround was defensive cover for the old broken-share/zone-corruption era; the reaper + workspace consolidation + Sync Health panel now prevent and surface that, so it's safe to use the correct policy. IMPORTANT: both Macs must be on v0.1.248 for edits to sync both directions.

item.title: Version 0.1.247
item.description: One-tap recovery in the Sync Health panel. Every issue the panel surfaces now has a Fix button that performs the recovery inline, reusing the existing confirmed flows: 'Turn on iCloud sync' (local-only fallback → enable + relaunch), 'Create share + copy link' (no live CKShare → mints the share and copies the invite, non-destructive), and 'Consolidate into shared workspace' (stranded records / wrong active workspace → moves records onto the share-rooted workspace, with confirmation). See the problem, tap the fix — no more hunting through settings for the right button.

item.title: Version 0.1.246
item.description: Sync Health panel — Settings > Sync, at the top. The diagnostic that would have turned today's multi-hour 'why are my leads local / which workspace is shared / is the share even alive' archaeology into one glance. A traffic-light verdict (Syncing normally / with issues / Sync is broken) plus: CloudKit-active-vs-local-fallback mode, a workspace census (each workspace's store, lead count, whether it holds the live CKShare, and accepted/pending participants), stranded-record count, per-store lead counts, and a plain-English issue list where each problem names its own fix ('No workspace has a live CKShare — use Copy invite link', 'N records attached to a non-shared workspace — Consolidate', 'The active workspace has 0 leads but another holds them', etc). One-tap Copy for the full report. Read-only; recompute on demand.

item.title: Version 0.1.245
item.description: Two-tier drafting is now part of the main system — automatic, no toggle. It runs on the link-light, high-volume early stages (Day 0 Immediate, Day 1 Unexpected Value) for a faster draft, while every stage that carries a critical CTA — a required link, coupon, or testimonial (Workshop Invite, Proof of Transformation, Survey + Check-in, Group Cohort Invite, One Question, Quick-Win Drop, Choice-Point Referral, Quarterly Nurture) — drafts single-call at MEDIUM reasoning so the CTA reliably lands. New FollowUpStage.hasCriticalCTA gate decides the route. The old experimental toggle is replaced by a global 'Force single-call drafting' escape hatch (Settings > AI > OpenAI > Advanced, off by default) for if you ever want to disable the fast path entirely.

item.title: Version 0.1.244
item.description: Workshop Invite (and every CTA stage) now actually includes its CTA. Bug: a Day-10 Workshop Invite draft referenced 'a workshop' but had NO Eventbrite link and closed with a generic 'hit reply' — the model conflated the required soft-CTA close with the primary CTA and dropped the real ask. Fix: stages with a hard call-to-action (workshop invite, discovery call, Alberto) now get a hoisted, self-checked REQUIRED-CTA block at the TOP of the prompt ('the close MUST be this CTA with its URL, not a hit-reply line; verify the link is present before output'). The soft-CTA-close section now explicitly defers to the primary CTA when one exists, so value-first stages still close soft but CTA stages close with the link + sign-up ask. Re-draft to pick it up.

item.title: Version 0.1.243
item.description: CKSyncEngine pilot — now an evaluation instrument. The Device Sync pilot (Settings > Sync, experimental) gains the telemetry that makes it actually useful for judging whether CKSyncEngine should replace NSPersistentCloudKitContainer: a live engine event log (fetch/send/conflict/state-persist), pending-to-send count, last fetch/send timestamps, and the headline migration metric — cross-device PROPAGATION LATENCY (how many seconds a change made on one device takes to appear on another). That's the number that decides the migration: CKSyncEngine in seconds vs NSPCKC's flaky minutes. Run it on two of your Macs, make a change, and watch the propagation time. Still fully isolated from your leads, off by default.

item.title: Version 0.1.242
item.description: Kills profile-viewing openers in follow-ups. Every 'I saw your profile' / 'I saw your background' / 'I checked out your profile' / 'came across your profile' / 'looked at your profile' framing is now hard-banned across all follow-up drafting paths (cold-lead guardrail, two-tier opener, Sales Nav voice rules). These read as a cold first-glance and are especially jarring in later funnel stages where you've already sent 2-3 messages. Replaced with DIRECT PERSONAL openers that work at any stage — '[N] years in [field], and [observation]' or 'As a [Creative Director] in this market, [observation]' — anchored on who the lead is, framed as something you bring to them, with no viewing verb. Re-draft your follow-ups (Leads selection mode > Draft Follow-ups, or the Re-draft button) to regenerate against the new openers.

item.title: Version 0.1.241
item.description: Day-5 Survey + Check-in is now real. The surveyCheckIn follow-up stage used to ship a dead [SURVEY_URL_PLACEHOLDER] — now it resolves to a real per-lead survey link. New: a branded 4-question survey page (survey-web/) you deploy to your existing Firebase project's Hosting; it reads the lead's stable token from the URL and writes the response to Firestore. The app derives the survey URL automatically from the Firebase project ID you already configured for the Alberto dashboard and bakes each lead's token in, so responses tie back to the lead. The token is a deterministic SHA-256 over stable lead fields (survives store rebuilds, no new schema). Until the survey site is deployed, drafts gracefully skip the survey sentence instead of shipping a placeholder or a fake link. Deploy steps: survey-web/README.md.

item.title: Version 0.1.240
item.description: Funnel-aware follow-ups + fresh start. (1) FUNNEL FIX: the bulk follow-up queue now drafts each lead's next UNSENT stage (Day 0 Immediate, Day 1 Unexpected Value, Day 4 Proof of Transformation, ...) instead of collapsing every lead older than ~14 days to the generic 'nurture' resting stage. The drafts now reference the real funnel stage; the prompt names the funnel + stage + day explicitly. Also fixed a hardcoded '14-Day Momentum funnel' header that mislabeled 30-Day Activation leads. (2) COLD OPENER FIX: killed 'I saw your background' / 'came across your profile' / 'caught my eye' at the source — the cold-lead guardrail was handing the model these as APPROVED examples while a separate patcher hunted them down. Replaced with the house opener 'I checked out your profile and I'm impressed by [specific thing].' (3) FRESH START: one-time wipe of every existing 'Follow-up Ready' green-bubble draft on this update, so you start clean with the new drafter. Pending drafts are un-sent, so this is non-destructive. (4) TEST A FEW AT A TIME: in Leads selection mode, a new 'Draft Follow-ups (N)' button generates funnel-aware drafts for just the checked leads instead of the whole due queue. (5) CKSyncEngine migration pilot in Settings > Sync (experimental, off by default) — isolated cross-device registry to evaluate the NSPCKC replacement.

item.title: Version 0.1.239
item.description: Teammate sync reliability. PRIMARY FIX: on macOS the foreground sync poller now keeps running while the app is open but not frontmost — it previously stopped the instant you clicked into another app, which (combined with macOS silent push being unreliable) meant a teammate's edits stopped flowing in whenever you were working elsewhere. macOS doesn't suspend non-frontmost apps, so the 20s poll now covers 'app open, Mac awake, working in another app' — the main 'my teammate's data isn't updating' case. Only pauses when all windows are closed / app hidden. iOS keeps stop-on-background (iOS genuinely suspends). SECONDARY FIX: accepting a CKShare invitation now re-registers the shared-database push subscription against the freshly-accepted share, so a participant who joins (or re-joins after a recovery) gets their shared subscription bound to the live share instead of stale/absent state. Note: silent push on a SLEEPING Mac still won't sync until wake — the poller covers awake-and-open; that's a macOS platform limit, not a bug.

item.title: Version 0.1.238
item.description: CRITICAL FIX: Install Update finally works on sandboxed builds. v0.1.236's entitlement+SUEnable fix got the download to complete, but Install still died with 'Failed to make auth right set / Failed copying system domain rights: -60005 / Failed to submit installer job'. Root cause: release script's deep re-sign was applying our sandboxed-host entitlements to every nested binary, forcing Sparkle's Installer.xpc into the sandbox. Installer.xpc MUST stay unsandboxed because it calls AuthorizationCreate to elevate to admin for /Applications writes — sandbox blocks that API. v0.1.238 rewrites the codesign step to hand-walk each nested binary with --preserve-metadata=entitlements, keeping each one's original entitlements (Sparkle's CI bake-in for the XPC services, Xcode for SalesNavCapture); only the host app gets --entitlements applied. Release script self-validates Installer.xpc is unsandboxed before shipping; aborts if not. The Downloader and host entitlement fixes from v0.1.236 stand. Manual install path still required for v0.1.234 users (their broken entitlements still block Install Update); future hops will auto-update cleanly.

item.title: Version 0.1.237
item.description: Two-tier follow-up drafting (opt-in behind hidden toggle). Settings > AI > OpenAI > Experimental > 'Two-tier follow-up drafting' splits Draft Follow-up into a parallel slow-opener (reasoning_effort: medium, ~50 tokens) + fast-body (reasoning_effort: minimal, ~750 tokens). Expected total latency drops ~30-50% on the 9 templated stages: immediate, unexpectedValue, proofOfTransformation, oneQuestion, workshopInvite, quickWinDrop, surveyCheckIn, groupCohortInvite, choicePointReferral, quarterlyAlumniNurture. The 5 skip-list stages (softBreakup, discoveryRecap, progressAuditTestimonial, nurture, Sales-Nav cold opener) stay on the existing single-call precision path regardless of the toggle. Drafter splices opener into body output right after 'Hey {firstName},' line; falls back to body-only if opener fails. Toggle defaults OFF so single-call stays default until A/B verified.

item.title: Version 0.1.236
item.description: Update diagnostics + Sparkle installer fix. CRITICAL: v0.1.234's Sparkle mach-lookup entitlements were guessing wrong service names (org.sparkle-project.*) — Sparkle 2 actually uses <bundle-id>-spki / -spks / -spkp derived from the host bundle ID (verified against Sparkle's own TestApplication entitlements). Also adds SUEnableInstallerLauncherService + SUEnableDownloaderService Info.plist keys that sandboxed apps must declare to opt into Sparkle's XPC services. Without both, Install Update silently falls back to in-process installation which the sandbox blocks ('An error occurred while launching the installer'). New SparkleUpdaterDiagnostics captures every Sparkle lifecycle event with full NSError chain. New SyncErrorInterpreter translates Cocoa error codes (134421 etc) into plain English, walks the underlying CKError chain, surfaces per-item partial failures. Recent Errors rows in the sync popover are now expandable to show the full interpreted chain inline. NOTE: Gorgeous's v0.1.234 has the broken entitlements so it can't auto-install this update — she needs a manual download + replace for this one hop; future updates will install cleanly.

item.title: Version 0.1.235
item.description: Self-heal for orphan CKShare zones. New BrokenShareZoneReaper observes NSPersistentCloudKitContainer events, detects CKError 26 (Zone Not Found) inside partial-failure events, and clears the dead zone reference via Apple's documented purgeObjectsAndRecordsInZone — breaking the endless retry loop you'd otherwise see when a CKShare was deleted server-side. Runs automatically in the background; Settings ▸ Sync also gets a Reap dead share zones button plus an Advanced disclosure for force-purging a specific zone UUID by hand.

item.title: Version 0.1.234
item.description: Recovery release. Adds Stop sharing entirely button (Settings ▸ Sync) to programmatically delete a stuck CKShare via CKDatabase.deleteRecord — bypasses Apple's flaky system Sharing UI. Fixes Copy invite link returning a URL before the readWrite publicPermission persisted to the server (cause of 'owner stopped sharing' errors on freshly-minted links). Adds Sparkle 2.x sandbox mach-lookup entitlements so the auto-updater installer can actually launch (cause of 'An error occurred while launching the installer' on previous updates).

item.title: Version 0.1.233
item.description: Fixes Copy invite link hang ('Swift task continuation misuse' — persistUpdatedShare's completion sometimes never fires when there's nothing to persist; we now short-circuit when the share already has a URL and use a 10s timeout + one-shot guard when an actual persist is needed). Also: Fireshot filename matching in bulk import.

item.title: Version 0.1.232
item.description: CRITICAL FIX: restores CloudKit sync after v0.1.229. The CODE_SIGN_INJECT_BASE_ENTITLEMENTS=NO setting (added for notarization to strip get-task-allow) also stripped application-identifier + team-identifier, which CKContainer requires. Symptom on /Applications install was 'Trying to initialize a container without an application ID' + no syncing to/from teammates. Also: Fireshot screenshot filenames now matched in bulk import; Email Proposal subject lines hoisted to position-0 + parser fallback; Gmail signature social icons use cached-URL fallback chain.

item.title: Version 0.1.231
item.description: Fixes launch crash on /Applications install — adds Mac framework rpath so dyld finds embedded Sparkle.framework. v0.1.229 + v0.1.230 had the rpath missing and would crash with 'Library not loaded: @rpath/Sparkle.framework' on any non-DerivedData launch.

channel.title: AM Creative CRM
channel.description: Sparkle update feed for AM Creative CRM.

item.title: Version 0.1.230
item.description: Fixes Gmail signature social icons (was missing the cached-URL fallback chain) and Email Proposal Subject line consistency (hoisted to position-0 of the addendum + parser fallback synthesizes one from the body when the model slips).

item.title: Version 0.1.229
item.description: First Sparkle build — auto-update + notarization, no more Gatekeeper prompts
